mechanism_narrative: The Fed's emergency rate cuts to near-zero (FEDFUNDS at 0.09) mechanically lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signal aggressive liquidity support, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice risk assets higher.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS cut to 0.09 from prior levels near 1.5-1.75 within weeks
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or SPX posts negative 20d returns in >55% of low-rate periods post-cut.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE beyond a 0.5pp threshold within a single reporting period signals an accelerating labor market collapse that will depress consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1 with lagged reporting amid rapid COVID-19 layoffs not yet fully captured
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following a >0.5pp UNRATE jump are positive in more than 50% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates a synchronized collapse in consumer and residential investment demand that reinforces recessionary expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444377 and HOUST at 1093 reflect pre-lockdown levels about to reverse sharply
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to outperform single-series RSAFS or HOUST models in out-of-sample Rank IC by any margin, or forward 20d returns are positive in >55% of joint-decline instances.

mechanism_narrative: During an active recession regime, a decline in INDPRO signals broad-based industrial output contraction driven by supply-chain disruption and demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations for industrial and cyclical sectors are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.1152 reported with one-month lag ahead of expected COVID-driven shutdown collapse
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when INDPRO declines during NBER-dated recessions, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls below 0.25% (effective zero lower bound) while CPIAUCSL growth remains subdued, markets interpret the policy stance as maximal accommodation with limited inflation risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term liquidity relief dominates price action.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09 and CPIAUCSL at 237.909 indicate zero-bound policy with contained inflation
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following zero-bound FEDFUNDS crossings are negative in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in PAYEMS reflects healthy labor income generation supporting consumption and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; this always-on relationship holds regardless of macro regime absent a payroll trend reversal.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139118 thousand reflects pre-shutdown employment level with one-month reporting lag
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS trailing 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deceleration in GDPC1 (quarterly real GDP) alongside declining RSAFS signals that aggregate demand weakness is broad-based and confirmed at both macro and micro consumption levels, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings downgrades cascade across sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15994.3 billion reported with a five-month lag, unable to reflect COVID-19 demand shock yet
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1-RSAFS decline signal fails to improve Rank IC over either series alone in out-of-sample tests, or forward 60d returns are positive in >55% of joint-decline instances.

mechanism_narrative: During an active monetary easing regime, a stable or declining CPIAUCSL alongside falling FEDFUNDS indicates that policymakers have room to sustain accommodation without inflation constraints, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk appetite recovers on disinflationary easing confirmation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.909 with no acceleration signal amid aggressive FEDFUNDS cuts
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with low inflation
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns during concurrent CPIAUCSL stability and FEDFUNDS declines are negative in more than 55% of historical easing-cycle instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.
